categories.name: Reentrancy
patterns: External call (.call, .transfer, .send, or external contract invocation) before state update (checks-effects-interactions violation)
Missing reentrancy guard on functions that transfer value or call external contracts
Cross-function reentrancy: shared state mutated by one function while another is mid-external-call

categories.name: Unchecked external call / silent failure
patterns: Return value of low-level .call() not checked, allowing silent failure
Assuming .transfer()/.send() success without handling the false/revert case
Delegatecall to untrusted or user-supplied address

categories.name: ABI encoding / interface mismatch
patterns: Function selector collision from mismatched signatures between interface and implementation
abi.encode vs abi.encodePacked used inconsistently between signing and verification, breaking signature checks
Struct field order mismatch between contract and off-chain encoding (client encodes differently than contract expects)
Mismatched parameter types between a proxy's low-level call and the implementation's expected calldata

categories.name: Storage layout / upgrade collision
patterns: Proxy pattern where implementation storage layout does not match proxy slot layout
Adding/reordering state variables in an upgrade, shifting existing storage slots
Missing storage gaps in upgradeable base contracts

categories.name: Access control gaps
patterns: Missing onlyOwner/role modifier on state-changing or fund-moving functions
tx.origin used for authorization instead of msg.sender
Initializer function callable multiple times or by any address (missing initializer guard)

categories.name: Arithmetic & precision
patterns: Division before multiplication causing precision loss
Unsafe casting between uint sizes truncating values
Rounding direction inconsistently favors the wrong party across similar functions

categories.name: Deployment / configuration drift
patterns: Hardcoded addresses that differ between testnet and mainnet without a config abstraction
Constructor/initializer args that silently default to zero/empty if not explicitly passed
Chain-ID-specific logic not guarded

categories.name: External verifier / oracle integration
patterns: Verifying key or trusted setup data truncated, padded, or malformed relative to what the proving system expects
Public inputs passed in wrong order or wrong type (array vs flat) to a verifier contract
No freshness check on oracle-supplied data before using it in a bounds check
